positioning_statement: For student pilots and flight instructors who are frustrated by fragmented, outdated tools that weren't designed for them, Abeam is a pilot-first platform that brings logbook, currency tracking, weather, scheduling, and progress into one modern app. Unlike Flight Schedule Pro and LogTen Pro, which serve school administrators or power users on a single platform, Abeam starts with the people actually flying — and grows with them from first lesson to flight school management.

personas.name: Student Pilot

demographics.description: College student or early career professional, pre-solo through checkride prep.
demographics.discovery: TikTok, Instagram, Reddit, YouTube, CFI recommendations

priorities: Simplicity (must be intuitive on first open)
pain_points: Juggling 5+ apps for logbook, weather, documents, scheduling, and study
No good free logbook with modern UX
Confusion about currency and medical requirements
Cost opacity — never sure how much training has actually cost
Instructor turnover means lost context

hooks: Free to fly with. No hour limits. No bait-and-switch.
Your logbook, your currency, your weather — one app, on your iPhone and the web
Built for the way you fly
Log a flight in under 30 seconds
messaging.elevator_pitch: Abeam is a free app that puts your whole flying life in one place — your logbook, your currency, live weather at your home airport, your aircraft, and your certificates. It works on your iPhone, your iPad, and the web, so your data is always with you. We built it because we're pilots too, and we were tired of juggling five different apps just to go flying.
messaging.emphasis: Emphasize FREE. Emphasize SIMPLE. Never talk down about experience level.

personas.tone: Warm, encouraging, peer-to-peer. Respectful of the financial and emotional weight of training. Never condescending. Enthusiastic without being cheesy.

pain_point_matrix.message: One app for your whole flying life
iOS, Android, and web. Your choice.
pain_point_matrix.retired_reason: Do not use — no Android app at launch

pain_point_matrix.message: Upload once. We track it for you.
Know what you've spent — and what's ahead
Your progress is yours — it stays with you

pain_point_matrix.pain: No way to see what's next
pain_point_matrix.message: Your dashboard shows where you are and what's coming

personas.name: Active Private Pilot

demographics.description: Working professional, PPL or instrument rated. Flies 2-8 times per month. Rents from a club or co-owns.

priorities: Value (does this replace something I already pay for?)
Quality (they've used ForeFlight — they expect polish)
pain_points: Currency anxiety ("I had to cancel a flight because I wasn't sure if my night currency was valid")
Subscription fatigue ($200/yr ForeFlight + $130/yr LogTen)
Platform lock-in (all data in ForeFlight/LogTen, can't leave)
ForeFlight's logbook is an afterthought bolted onto flight planning

hooks: Never wonder 'am I current?' again
Your data follows you. Not the other way around.
Your logbook shouldn't cost more than avgas
messaging.emphasis: Understated, factual. They know what they want — don't oversell.

personas.tone: Peer-to-peer, factual and fair about competitors, understated. Respect their time and intelligence.
channels: In-person at fly-ins and EAA meetings

pain_point_matrix.pain: A pilot app that doesn't care what phone you carry
pain_point_matrix.message: Finally, a pilot app that doesn't care what phone you carry
pain_point_matrix.retired_reason: Do not use — implies Android support

personas.name: Flight School Owner

demographics.description: Former CFI or commercial pilot. 1-10 aircraft, 5-15 instructors, 30-80 active students. $300K-$2M annual revenue.
demographics.discovery: Events, email, aviation forums, in-person at aviation events

pain_points: Scheduling is a mess and planes sit idle
More time on admin than flying or teaching
Current software too expensive, too complicated, or desktop-only
Opaque pricing ("Why do I have to talk to sales?")

hooks: Modern software designed for Part 61 schools
Your people are already on Abeam. Now you can connect them.
ROI-oriented: time saved, utilization improved, transparent pricing
messaging.elevator_pitch: Abeam is a modern platform for flight schools that your instructors and students actually want to use — because many of them already do. We start with free pilot tools, add instructor management, and then give schools scheduling, billing, and fleet management at transparent pricing with no contracts. Your people are already on Abeam. Now you can connect them.
messaging.emphasis: Thin margins — every dollar counts. Lead with ROI and transparent pricing.

personas.tone: Professional but not corporate. Respect for aviation expertise. Peer-to-peer, not vendor-to-customer.

personas.name: Flight School Manager / Chief Instructor

demographics.description: Experienced CFI with 1,000-5,000 hours. Manages day-to-day operations. Key influencer — if they don't like it, the purchase doesn't happen.

pain_points: Half the day spent managing schedules (phone calls, texts, whiteboard updates)
Instructors don't update availability
No centralized student progression tracking
Desktop-only systems that can't be used from the flight line

hooks: Get back to teaching instead of coordinating schedules
Update schedules from anywhere — you're not chained to the desk
Students can see availability and book themselves — no more phone tag
There's a better way to run your CFI business
The first app built for the way you actually work
Get your non-flying hours back
See every student's journey at a glance
messaging.elevator_pitch: Abeam starts as a pilot logbook and dashboard, but we're building a full instructor platform — student management, digital endorsements, scheduling, debriefs, billing. Right now your students can use Abeam Pilot for free, and when you upgrade to Abeam Instruct, you get instructor tools that were actually built for how independent CFIs work. No more Google Calendar and Venmo.
messaging.emphasis: Practical, solutions-oriented. Concise. Empathetic to daily challenges. Empathize with the admin burden. Acknowledge thin margins. Speak to the reality of independent instruction.

personas.tone: Practical, solutions-oriented. Concise. Empathetic to daily challenges. Professional but warm.

pain_point_matrix.message: There's a better way to run your CFI business
Digital endorsements. Stored properly. Always accessible.
The first app built for the way you actually work

pain_point_matrix.pain: No way to share materials with students
pain_point_matrix.message: Your resource portal — docs, tools, and materials in one link
See every student's journey at a glance
Built-in invoicing — no more chasing checks

personas.name: Independent CFI / Small Flying Club Manager

demographics.description: CFI building hours or volunteer club manager. 1-2 aircraft, 5-15 students or 30-150 club members. Extremely price-sensitive.

priorities: DIY mindset — willing to figure things out themselves
pain_points: Manual billing eats hours every month
Enterprise software costs more than they make
Scheduling via group texts and shared Google calendars
"All-in-one" solutions have features they don't need (paying for bloat)

hooks: Stop spending hours on manual billing and scheduling
Set up your account in minutes, not days
Month-to-month, cancel anytime
messaging.emphasis: Lead with price. Show the $29/month number early.

personas.tone: No-nonsense, respectful of budget constraints, supportive.

investor_press_pitch: Abeam is a platform for general aviation growing from the bottom up. We give pilots free tools — logbook, currency, weather — then expand into paid instructor and flight school management. Our go-to-market flips the industry: instead of selling to school admins and forcing adoption, we build loyalty with individuals first. The flight training software market is fragmented, outdated, and ripe for a modern, mobile-first platform.
writing_guidelines: Check the persona's pain points — lead with one they'd recognize.
Match their tone expectations (student = warm; private pilot = understated; school owner = professional).
Use their preferred channels' formatting conventions.
Reference their buying behavior to calibrate the CTA (students: "free, no credit card"; school owners: "schedule a demo or try free for 30 days").
